title: Do Your Laundry
summary: Sort clothes, choose the right washer settings, dry them properly, and fold so they are ready to wear.
learnerPromise: Wash, dry, and fold clothes without shrinking or dyeing them.
whyItMatters: Clean clothes feel better to wear and last longer. Learning the basics of laundry keeps your favorite items in good shape and prevents accidents like shrunken sweaters or pink-tinted whites.
realLifeUses: Wash everyday clothes so they stay fresh
Save a favorite shirt from being ruined by hot water
Dry clothes efficiently without overloading the machine
Fold clothes so they are easy to find and put away
youWillLearn: How to sort clothes by color and fabric type
How to read clothing care labels
Which washer and dryer settings match your load
How to fold common items like shirts and pants
miniChallenge: Wash one small load from start to finish, including drying and folding.
steps: Gather dirty clothes and sort them into piles: whites, darks, and delicates.
Check the care labels on a few items to confirm water temperature and dryer safety.
Load one pile into the washing machine, add detergent, and pick the matching cycle.
Move the washed clothes to the dryer or hang them to air dry if the label suggests it.
When dry, fold each item neatly and place it in a stack or basket to put away.
completionCriteria: One complete load is washed, dried, and folded
No items were damaged by incorrect settings
commonProblems: Colors bled onto lighter clothes — wash darks and whites separately
Clothes came out shrunk — check labels and use cold water or air dry
Detergent left residue — use the recommended amount, not extra
tips: Turn printed t-shirts inside out before washing to protect the design
Remove clothes from the dryer slightly early to prevent wrinkles
Keep a small hamper or bin in your room so dirty clothes have a home
